old_claim.note: Hospital Patient Wards provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Earlier organized hospital wards supplied the institutional care model adapted to a dedicated plague-isolation hospital.

source_shape.source_locator: The City of Venice distinguishes the 1423 hospital for infected patients from the 1468 preventive quarantine station for people and goods.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Venice created Lazzaretto Vecchio in 1423 as a dedicated hospital for people infected with plague.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The chronology supports predecessor lineage rather than a one-to-one enabling dependency. Organized wards are institutional hospital lineage; the Venetian island hospital was a distinct civic isolation facility.

ontology_before: hospital_patient_wards was modeled as an enabling capability for plague_pesthouses.
ontology_after: hospital_patient_wards is retained as historical lineage for plague_pesthouses, not as a necessary implementation capability.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-12-structural-accuracy-batch: plague_pesthouses->hospital_patient_wards is historical_predecessor.
would_reject_if: A reliable source demonstrates that the scoped plague_pesthouses implementation technically requires hospital_patient_wards.
short_reason: Organized wards are institutional hospital lineage; the Venetian island hospital was a distinct civic isolation facility.
